page: OVHcloud h100-760 is a GPU-accelerated server powered by an AMD EPYC 9354 processor with 60 dedicated physical cores operating at 3.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 760.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.67 GB per core. Acceleration is delivered via two NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs, supplying a combined 160 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 4040 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is rated at a 16 Gbps baseline.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 156,986.02 and a PassMark CPU score of 93,218.13. Large-block memory bandwidth is average, measuring 172.41 GB/sec for read operations, but small-block cached read bandwidth reaches a top-tier 8,491.57 GB/sec. Large-model LLM inference benchmarks demonstrate top-tier capabilities, achieving 727.7 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 29.48 tokens/sec for text generation on a 70B parameter model.

The server represents a high-density resource profile where the cost efficiency is aligned with intensive parallel computing demands rather than general-purpose hosting. While single-threaded decompression and large-block memory writes perform at average levels, the dedicated CPU allocation and dual H100 GPUs make it highly efficient for large language model inference, deep learning training, database operations, and complex physics simulations.
description: OVHcloud h100-760 is a high-performance, GPU-accelerated server designed for intensive parallel computing workloads. It features an AMD EPYC 9354 processor with 60 dedicated physical cores running at 3.0 GHz, paired with 760.0 GB of system memory. The server is equipped with two NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs providing a total of 160 GB VRAM, alongside 4040 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 16 Gbps network baseline. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional LLM inference speeds, processing 727.7 tokens/sec for prompts on a 70B model. While large-block memory write speeds and single-threaded operations perform at average levels, the dedicated hardware profile delivers high resource density. This server is optimized for large-scale artificial intelligence inference, deep learning, database operations, and high-performance computing tasks.
og_description: OVHcloud h100-760 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 60 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 760 GB RAM, 4040 GB NVMe storage, and two NVIDIA H100 GPUs with 160 GB total VRAM for demanding AI and HPC workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud h100-760 is a GPU-accelerated server with 60 AMD EPYC cores, 760 GB RAM, and dual NVIDIA H100 GPUs for AI inference and HPC workloads.
tagline: A dual-GPU accelerated platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and NVMe storage for high-performance artificial intelligence and parallel computing.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs with 160 GB total VRAM
60 dedicated AMD EPYC 9354 physical cores at 3.0 GHz
760 GB system memory and 4040 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier LLM inference performance across small, medium, and large models
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM hypervisor virtualization
Best suited for AI model training, large-scale inference, and database operations
